texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membrane technology for efficient nitrogen generation
texts.text: Membrane technology for efficient nitrogen generation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Evonik, the creative industrial group from Germany, is one of the world leaders in specialty chemicals. As a technological leader in the field of high-performance polymers we offer hollow-fiber membranes for efficient and energy-saving gas separation.
texts.text: Evonik, the creative industrial group from Germany, is one of the world leaders in specialty chemicals. As a technological leader in the field of high-performance polymers we offer hollow-fiber membranes for efficient and energy-saving gas separation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · No other auxiliary materials, such as water and sorbents, required
texts.text: No other auxiliary materials, such as water and sorbents, required

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Functioning of a membrane module for nitrogen generation
texts.text: Functioning of a membrane module for nitrogen generation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membranes for gas separation are produced in the form of hollow fibers by a phase-inversion process. Gas separation membranes work on the principle of selective permeation through a membrane surface. The driving force for gas permeation through the membrane is the difference between the partial pressures of the gas on the retentate side (the internal side of the hollow fiber) and the permeate (external) side. The greater this difference, the greater the permeation of gas through the membrane.
texts.text: Membranes for gas separation are produced in the form of hollow fibers by a phase-inversion process. Gas separation membranes work on the principle of selective permeation through a membrane surface. The driving force for gas permeation through the membrane is the difference between the partial pressures of the gas on the retentate side (the internal side of the hollow fiber) and the permeate (external) side. The greater this difference, the greater the permeation of gas through the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the separation of oxygen and nitrogen, for example, permeation of oxygen through the membrane is strongly favored while nitrogen is retained.
texts.text: In the separation of oxygen and nitrogen, for example, permeation of oxygen through the membrane is strongly favored while nitrogen is retained.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The higher the selectivity, the higher the energy efficiency of the resulting membrane process.
texts.text: The higher the selectivity, the higher the energy efficiency of the resulting membrane process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The permeation rate of each gas depends on its solubility in the membrane material as well as on its diffusion rate.
texts.text: The permeation rate of each gas depends on its solubility in the membrane material as well as on its diffusion rate.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Gases with higher solubility and lower molecular size penetrate the membrane faster than large, less soluble gases. The ratio of the transport speeds of two gases is termed selectivity.
texts.text: Gases with higher solubility and lower molecular size penetrate the membrane faster than large, less soluble gases. The ratio of the transport speeds of two gases is termed selectivity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Different membrane materials show different separation characteristics. Evonik's membrane polymer, especially tailored for nitrogen generation, is characterized by both high permeability and high selectivity.
texts.text: Different membrane materials show different separation characteristics. Evonik's membrane polymer, especially tailored for nitrogen generation, is characterized by both high permeability and high selectivity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The SEPURAN® product family includes a number of different membrane systems for gas separation in a variety of applications. SEPURAN® N 2 has been specially developed for nitrogen generation.
texts.text: The SEPURAN® product family includes a number of different membrane systems for gas separation in a variety of applications. SEPURAN® N 2 has been specially developed for nitrogen generation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: SEPURAN® membrane modules consist of several thousand hollow fibers produced from high-performance polymers.
texts.text: SEPURAN® membrane modules consist of several thousand hollow fibers produced from high-performance polymers.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The fibers are bundled together in a stainless steel tube and their ends embedded in a resin.
texts.text: The fibers are bundled together in a stainless steel tube and their ends embedded in a resin.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As many membrane modules as required may be linked together, depending on the particular application and the size of the system. This simple and modular setup, with minimal space requirements in the air separation facility, offers a unique advantage comparing to other technologies.
texts.text: As many membrane modules as required may be linked together, depending on the particular application and the size of the system. This simple and modular setup, with minimal space requirements in the air separation facility, offers a unique advantage comparing to other technologies.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Our SEPURAN® N 2 module can be used in a very wide range of applications. In comparison with other membranes available, SEPURAN® N 2 combines excellent capacity with low air consumption.
texts.text: Our SEPURAN® N 2 module can be used in a very wide range of applications. In comparison with other membranes available, SEPURAN® N 2 combines excellent capacity with low air consumption.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Alternatively, at low flow rates our SEPURAN® N 2 Selective module can achieve high purity with the lowest air consumption. The energy requirement is particularly low while the capacity remains competitive.
texts.text: Alternatively, at low flow rates our SEPURAN® N 2 Selective module can achieve high purity with the lowest air consumption. The energy requirement is particularly low while the capacity remains competitive.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For more specific information, please ask for our Flyer 'SEPURAN® N 2 Selective'.
texts.text: For more specific information, please ask for our Flyer 'SEPURAN® N 2 Selective'.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: High capacity combined with low air consumption
texts.text: High capacity combined with low air consumption

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Evonik's SEPURAN® N 2 Module has a high selectivity and a high permeability which combines a low air consumption with high nitrogen capacity. This leads, amongst other advantages, to low energy costs, a small compressor and few modules.
texts.text: Evonik's SEPURAN® N 2 Module has a high selectivity and a high permeability which combines a low air consumption with high nitrogen capacity. This leads, amongst other advantages, to low energy costs, a small compressor and few modules.

texts.orig: · More nitrogen production at the same air consumption
texts.text: More nitrogen production at the same air consumption

texts.text: The system can be started and stopped at short intervals, ensuring high flexibility

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · High capacity combined with low air consumption
texts.text: High capacity combined with low air consumption

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Cost effective air separation and nitrogen generation using SEPURAN® N 2 is particularly useful for blanketing and inerting. Reduction of atmospheric oxygen results in a large number of applications.
texts.text: Cost effective air separation and nitrogen generation using SEPURAN® N 2 is particularly useful for blanketing and inerting. Reduction of atmospheric oxygen results in a large number of applications.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The composition of the product was determined by measurement of the residual oxygen content.
texts.text: The composition of the product was determined by measurement of the residual oxygen content.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The 'N 2 produced' value is the inert gas content. The 'inlet air' value divided by N 2 produced gives the air factor or air/N 2 ratio.
texts.text: The 'N 2 produced' value is the inert gas content. The 'inlet air' value divided by N 2 produced gives the air factor or air/N 2 ratio.
